text.title: API Monitoring — Structured Logging Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Are logs still emitted as plain text or unstructured?

conclusion.verdict: switch to JSON logger with mandatory fields

branch.question: Are request_id fields missing from inbound logs?

conclusion.verdict: add a middleware to generate/propagate X-Request-ID and include in every log

branch.question: Are logs only on local disk?

conclusion.verdict: wire fluentbit/vector to ship to the central index

branch.question: Are tokens or PII appearing in log lines?

conclusion.verdict: install redaction middleware; rotate exposed credentials
drop level to INFO; reserve DEBUG for local dev

branch.question: Is the runtime air-gapped with no log shipping possible?

conclusion.verdict: structured logs without an index don't justify the cost
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
